name: New York
notes_normalized: Cannabis is legal in New York under state law for recreational adult use. Medical cannabis is also legal under a regulated state program in New York. Cannabis remains federally illegal in United States. Possession is limited to Up to 3 oz flower or 24g concentrate.
notes_raw: style="background:#196E92;" | {{Hs|1}} Smoking marijuana in public is subject to the same enforcement and penalties as smoking tobacco. In an amendment to the Boylan Bill, they added "Cannabis indica, which is the Indian hemp from which the East Indian drug called hashish is manufactured," to the city's list of restricted drugs. Whitebread note that "only four articles about marijuana appeared in the major New York newspaper during the entire period from 1914 until 1927." In 1927, New York removed the medical purposes and restricted cannabis completely. The Brooklyn Public Library reports: this group was held to a high moral standard and was prohibited from "entering saloons, using foul language, and neglecting horses." The Squad found the most weed in Queens but even in Brooklyn dug up "millions of dollars" worth of the plants, many as "tall as Christmas trees". In 1973, New York governor Nelson Rockefeller signed legislation increasing the penalty for selling or more of heroin, morphine, "raw or prepared opium," cocaine, or cannabis or possessing or more of the same substances, was a minimum of 15 years to life in prison, and a maximum of 25 years to life in prison. Possession of less than of marijuana, in any form, is unlawful possession of marijuana, punishable by a fine of no more than $100 if the defendant has no convictions for the offense within the last three years. Amounts in the range are fourth-degree criminal possession of marijuana, a Class A misdemeanor applies to amounts between, or one pound. Those convicted of second-degree criminal possession of marijuana, a Class D felony with a maximum sentence of seven years, will have been in possession of up to and can expect to serve up to seven years at most. First-degree criminal possession of marijuana applies to those with more than 10 pounds, a Class C felony for which offenders may spend 15 years in prison. Offenses related to the sale of marijuana start with fifth-degree criminal sale of marijuana, a class B misdemeanor that covers amounts less than.

facts.possession_limit: Up to 3 oz flower or 24g concentrate

state_modifiers.federal_conflict: Cannabis remains federally illegal in United States.
